definition.name: def-jordan-coboundary
definition.definition: Let v: B -> A map a JB-algebra B into the algebra A, with module action a.m := v(a) circ m (the ADJOINT module: the action is left Jordan multiplication). The multiplicativity defect g(a,b)=v(a circ b)-v(a) circ v(b) is a symmetric Jordan 2-cochain. The Jordan coboundary of a 1-cochain h is (d^1 h)(a,b) = v(a) circ h(b) + h(a) circ v(b) - h(a circ b). A derivation is an element of ker d^1. An exact-adjoint coboundary splitting (right inverse) is a linear map S: im(d^1) -> C^1 with d^1 S g = g for all g in im(d^1); its splitting constant K bounds ||S g|| <= K ||g|| in the injective order-unit cochain norm.
